### Every Few Minutes

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 30 min. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Fetch Target Page

### Fetch Target Page

GET https://example-market.com/events — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Extract Listings (CSS) · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Extract Listings (CSS)

Extracts structured values from raw HTML via CSS selectors.

Next: Rows from Arrays

### Rows from Arrays

Custom JavaScript: Zip parallel arrays into one item per listing

Next: Diff vs Last Run

### Load Previous Snapshot

Reads every row from 'Event Tracker' — the read side of the store, e.g. for a digest or a downstream sync.

Ports: ①→ Diff vs Last Run · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Diff vs Last Run

Diffs two streams by key into 4 lanes: only-in-A, same, different, only-in-B — the heart of the sync.

Ports: ①→ Alert New Findings · ③→ Update Changed Rows · ④→ Write Fresh Snapshot

### Alert New Findings

Posts to a Discord channel via an incoming webhook — no bot token needed, the webhook URL on the credential is the only secret.

Ports: ①→ Merge Diff Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Discord webhook URL

### Update Changed Rows

Upserts a row into 'Event Tracker', matched on `eventId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Ports: ①→ Merge Diff Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Write Fresh Snapshot

Appends a new row to 'Event Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Merge Diff Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Merge Diff Outcomes

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Enrich with Reference Data

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Row Snapshot

Appends a new row to 'Event Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Summarize Run Stats · ②→ Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Event Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

# Alert on stock levels with HTTP / REST API and Discord on autopilot

**Pattern:** Threshold Monitoring · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Alert on stock levels with HTTP / REST API and Discord on autopilot. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual escalations is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Every Few Minutes → Fetch Target Page → Extract Listings (CSS) → Rows from Arrays → Diff vs Last Run → Alert New Findings → Merge Diff Outcomes → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to threshold monitoring without manual effort
- Anyone automating escalations in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Saves hours of manual work each week
- Runs 24/7 in the background

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Discord webhook URL; Google OAuth2 / service account.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Every run diffs against the previous snapshot instead of re-alerting on everything scraped — without this, the workflow would spam the same unchanged listings on every scheduled run. The three-way split (new / changed / unchanged) means each gets exactly the treatment it needs: alert, update, or a silent snapshot refresh.

## Practice exercises

Already production-grade — deepen your understanding:

1. Change the CSS selectors to scrape a different site's listing structure.
2. Manually edit one row in the snapshot store and confirm it's flagged as changed on the next run.
3. Add a price-drop-specific alert condition on top of the generic new/changed diff.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your escalations process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
